meaning: adj.以羊毛盖上的,蓬松的,羊毛似的
examples: It was a lovely afternoon with a blue sky and a few fleecy white clouds.
A fleecy cloud was drifting past.
Dear little lambs in your fleecy fold.
The southern states, virtually devoid of non-slave labour, relied on the people flocking to the mills of Lancashire to turn their fleecy raw material into finished cloth.
